foundation.theorem: one machine-checked self-proven theorem: there is no nothing
foundation.trace: One -> fold -> binary count 2 and 24-state axis -> two 12-path mode frontiers; exact alpha/beta angles -> signed quartet modes; preserved mode frontiers -> complete sequence topology selection

mode_preservation.capacity_derivation: 24 lattice-axis states / binary_count() = 12 paths per generated orientation mode
mode_preservation.initial_census: the two exact circular bisectors are assigned to opposite 12-state halves of the psi axis, producing 12 alpha and 12 beta candidates
mode_preservation.within_mode_order: signed-orbit distance, terminal quartet topology, complete-prefix topology, state tuple
mode_preservation.cross_mode_order: complete-prefix topology, signed-orbit distance and terminal quartet topology, state tuple
mode_preservation.purpose: prevent either forced orientation mode from being erased before sequence-wide generated topology compares the preserved continuations

signed_orientation_relation.source: selector v6 immutable generated alpha/beta signatures
signed_orientation_relation.geometric_readout: dimensionless signed scalar triple product of generated C-alpha steps

component_routes.alpha_beta_angles: DIRECT exact fold preimages in constants/protein_folding_3d.ep
component_routes.balanced_frontier: CONSTITUTIONAL exact partition of the counted frontier by the binary count

status: development relation awaiting source-bound applied protein data
